auction_style: The Diamond Hacks are the expansion team that joined in 2022, recruited by Brian Garber. Andy Enzweiler is in graphic design (Arizona) and Ed O'Brien is an attorney (Tempe, AZ). Ed explicitly stated he values bullpen very little — 'Diamondbacks-White Sox fan so bullpen-agnostic, historically' — suggesting RP is deprioritized at auction. Ed also revealed he made a catastrophic trade in a previous league (Semien for Plesac in Month 1), meaning Ed hands off all trades to Andy. They appear to approach the league with enthusiasm and humor rather than deep preparation.
price_tendency_detail: Only 3 threads available, none with direct auction pricing data. Ed's stated bullpen-agnostic philosophy and rookie league membership suggests they may underspend on closers and relievers.
trade_style: Unknown from limited data. Ed explicitly said Andy handles all trades after his own disastrous trade history. The team is enthusiastic and social in group emails but no direct trade negotiations are captured.
notable_tendencies: Ed O'Brien is the league's comedian — Barry called him 'the Chris Rock of the Teddy Ballgame League'
Ed handles no trades: 'my co-manager will handle all trades here' — Andy is the trading partner
Ed is explicitly bullpen-agnostic: 'Diamondbacks-White Sox fan so bullpen-agnostic, historically'
Expansion team since 2022 — still developing pattern recognition within this specific league
Ed's previous league: sold 'never gonna last' Semien for Plesac in Month 1 — self-aware about bad decisions
Based in Tempe, AZ — in Mountain Time, 3 hours behind draft start; may be disadvantaged in live auction pace
intel_summary: The Diamond Hacks are a fun team that has yet to challenge for a title. Ed's bullpen-agnostic philosophy is a clear exploitable signal — they likely underspend on closers and RPs, which could create waiver/trade opportunities. Andy handles all in-season moves so direct your trade outreach to him. As a team still learning the league's rhythms, they may make mispriced nominations at the auction that benefit the table.
